zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Douglas 6th Lord of Drumlanrig於1481年3月10日出生在蘇格蘭度福利爾德盧姆藍利 卡素時，他的父親Sir James Douglas 5th Lord of Drumlanrig33歲，母親Janet Scott27歲。他於1501年與Elizabeth Gordon of Lochinvar在蘇格蘭金加迪爾格倫維結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1513年9月9日在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德布藍斯頓享年32歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾格倫維。

nameMeanings.name: Douglas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from any of various places called from their situation on a river named with Gaelic dubh ‘dark, black’ + glas ‘stream’ (a derivative of glas ‘blue’). There are several localities in Scotland and Ireland so named, but the one from which the surname is derived in most if not all cases is Douglas in Lanarkshire 20 miles south of Glasgow, the original stronghold of the influential Douglas family and their retainers.

History: The family taking their name from Douglas in Lanarkshire were of Flemish origin. They rose to great prominence in the 14th and 15th centuries, controlling the earldoms of Douglas, Morton, and Angus, and later, Queensberry.

variants.name: Douglass
meanings: Scottish and English (Durham and Northumbria): variant of   Douglas  .

History: William Douglass, a physician recognized for his identification and description of an epidemic of scarlet fever, was born c. 1691 in Gifford, Haddington County, Scotland, and settled in Boston in 1718. The abolitionist, orator, and journalist Frederick Douglass assumed the name after escaping from slavery in 1838 and traveling to Massachusetts. Son of a white father and a slave with some Indian blood, he was born Frederick Augustus Washington Bailey c. 1817 in Tuckahoe, MD.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Douglas 6th Lord of Drumlanrig was born on 10 March 1481, in Drumlanrig Castle, Dumfriesshire, Scotland, his father, Sir James Douglas 5th Lord of Drumlanrig, was 33 and his mother, Janet Scott, was 27. He married Elizabeth Gordon of Lochinvar in 1501, in Glenbervie, Kincardineshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died on 9 September 1513, in Branxton, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 32, and was buried in Glenbervie, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William Douglas 6th Lord of Drumlanrig est né le 10 mars 1481, à Drumlanrig Castle, Dumfriesshire, Écosse, son père, Sir James Douglas 5th Lord of Drumlanrig, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Janet Scott, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Gordon of Lochinvar en 1501, à Glenbervie, Kincardineshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 9 septembre 1513, à Branxton, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 32 ans, et a été enterré à Glenbervie, Aberdeenshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

Er hat 1501 in Glenbervie, Kincardineshire, Scotland Elizabeth Gordon of Lochinvar geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 9. September 1513 im Alter von 32 Jahren in Branxton, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Glenbervie, Aberdeenshire, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William Douglas 6th Lord of Drumlanrigが1481年3月10日にスコットランドダムフリーシュアイアドルムランリグ カストルで生まれた時，父Sir James Douglas 5th Lord of Drumlanrigは33歳，母Janet Scottは27歳であった。1501年にスコットランドキンカーディンエシュアイアグレンバーヴィでElizabeth Gordon of Lochinvarと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1513年9月9日にイギリスイギリスノースウンバーランドブランクストンで32歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアグレンバーヴィにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Douglas 6th Lord of Drumlanrig(이)가 1481년 3월 10일 스컷랜드 드프리실르 드럼랜리그 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir James Douglas 5th Lord of Drumlanrig(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Janet Scott(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1501년 스컷랜드 킨카딘실르 글렌버비에서 Elizabeth Gordon of Lochinvar(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1513년 9월 9일 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 브랜스턴에서 32세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 압르디실르 글렌버비에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Douglas 6th Lord of Drumlanrig nasceu em 10 de março de 1481, em Drumlanrig Castle, Dumfriesshire, Scotland, seu pai, Sir James Douglas 5th Lord of Drumlanrig, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Janet Scott, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Gordon of Lochinvar em 1501, em Glenbervie, Kincardineshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 9 de setembro de 1513, em Branxton, Northumberland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 32 anos, e foi sepultado em Glenbervie, Aberdeenshire, Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Douglas 6th Lord of Drumlanrig родился 10 марта 1481 г. в местности друмланриг кастл, думфриессхир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Sir James Douglas 5th Lord of Drumlanrig было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Janet Scott, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Gordon of Lochinvar в 1501 г. в местности гленбирви, кинкардаинешир, Шотландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер 9 сентября 1513 г. в местности бранкстон, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 32 лет и был похоронен в местности гленбирви, абирдиншир, Шотландия, Великобритания.

Se casó con Elizabeth Gordon of Lochinvar en 1501, en Glenbervie, Kincardineshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas.
